dsl_rule.translation_notes: Parallel trend channels approximated using Bollinger Bands (bb_upper/bb_lower as channel boundaries) and SMA_20 (trend direction confirmation). Entry conditions require price within channel and trend confirmation via ADX. Exit conditions trigger on boundary breach (upper/lower band) or trend structure break (crossing SMA in opposite direction). Logic applies to both uptrend (long) and downtrend (short) contexts as specified. Position sizing/defensive approach implicit in exit-at-boundary rules.

backtest_rule.reason: Parallel trend channels approximated using Bollinger Bands (bb_upper/bb_lower as channel boundaries) and SMA_20 (trend direction confirmation). Entry conditions require price within channel and trend confirmation via ADX. Exit conditions trigger on boundary breach (upper/lower band) or trend structure break (crossing SMA in opposite direction). Logic applies to both uptrend (long) and downtrend (short) contexts as specified. Position sizing/defensive approach implicit in exit-at-boundary rules.

source_rule_text: Use parallel trend channels to identify zones of heightened reversal risk and volatility; reduce long exposure or take profits when price approaches the upper channel boundary in uptrends, and apply inverse logic to downtrends.
